title: Synthetic Cybersecurity Onboarding
disclaimer: This is synthetic demo content created for the Agents League hackathon. It contains no real company policy, customer data, employee data, tenant content, or confidential information.

rooms.title: The Inbox Vault
rooms.subtitle: A countdown pulses over three suspicious messages.

sceneObjects.label: Mail Terminal
sceneObjects.description: Three emails flicker on the terminal. One mixes urgency, an unfamiliar sender, and a credential request.

sceneObjects.label: Hotline Clock
sceneObjects.description: The lock face is marked with a one-hour reporting window and a red emergency channel.

sceneObjects.label: Reporting Poster
sceneObjects.description: A laminated poster says routine phishing reports go to the security portal.

clues.label: Suspicious Email
clues.content: The payroll message asks for MFA codes, uses an unfamiliar domain, and demands action before lunch.

clues.label: Clock Etching
clues.content: Suspected phishing must be reported within one hour. Active credential theft needs immediate phone escalation.

clues.label: Portal Label
clues.content: The security reporting portal is the default channel for suspected phishing messages.

puzzle.title: Hotline Sequence Lock
puzzle.prompt: Build the correct response sequence for the suspicious payroll email.
puzzle.instructions: Select the steps in order. Use Clear if you need to rebuild the sequence.

steps.label: Report in portal within one hour
steps.detail: Use the approved security reporting portal for suspected phishing.

steps.label: Delete the message silently
steps.detail: Removes the email but leaves the security team blind.

steps.label: Identify phishing indicators
steps.detail: Check sender, urgency, links, credential requests, and attachments.

steps.label: Phone-escalate active credential theft
steps.detail: Use only when credentials were entered or theft is in progress.

citations.label: Phishing Reporting Policy, section 3.1
citations.snippet: Suspected phishing messages must be reported through the security reporting portal within one hour of discovery.

citations.label: Phishing Reporting Policy, section 3.2
citations.snippet: Employees should preserve the message and avoid forwarding it outside the approved reporting workflow.

citations.label: Incident Escalation Policy, section 2.2
citations.snippet: Evidence of active credential theft requires immediate phone escalation to the security response line.

hints.label: Look at the terminal first
hints.text: The first move is not a channel choice. Decide whether the message has phishing indicators.

hints.label: Portal before emergency line
hints.text: Routine suspected phishing uses the portal. The phone line is for evidence that credentials are already being stolen.

hints.label: Read the clock
hints.text: The cited policy says suspected phishing is reported through the portal within one hour of discovery.

hints.label: Answer explanation
hints.text: The valid sequence is identify indicators, report in the portal within one hour, then phone-escalate only if credential theft is active.

puzzle.successMessage: The hotline clock clicks open. You preserved the evidence and used the right escalation path.
puzzle.failureMessage: The lock rejects the sequence. The policy wants evidence first, then the approved reporting workflow.

rooms.exitCondition: Unlock the incident hotline by ordering the response steps.

rooms.title: The Data Locker
rooms.subtitle: Four drawers wait for the right classification labels.
learningObjectives: Keep restricted data in approved storage
Avoid exposing confidential material to public tools

sceneObjects.label: Drawer Bank
sceneObjects.description: The drawer labels read Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted.

sceneObjects.label: Sharing Badge
sceneObjects.description: A badge reader accepts only approved storage destinations for sensitive artifacts.

sceneObjects.label: Sealed Envelope
sceneObjects.description: The envelope warns that customer records and credentials never belong in public AI tools.

clues.label: Classification Labels
clues.content: Public information is approved for broad release, internal data is company-use only, confidential data could harm people or business if exposed, and restricted data has the highest handling controls.

clues.label: Storage Rule
clues.content: Confidential and restricted artifacts must stay in approved protected storage, not personal drives or public tools.

clues.label: AI Tool Warning
clues.content: Customer records, secrets, access tokens, and unreleased financials must not be pasted into public AI tools.

puzzle.title: Classification Drawer Lock
puzzle.prompt: Place each artifact into the correct policy drawer before the locker opens.
puzzle.instructions: Choose one classification for each artifact. Every artifact must be classified.

artifacts.label: Published security blog post
artifacts.detail: Already reviewed and posted on the public website.

artifacts.label: Team launch roadmap
artifacts.detail: Internal planning dates for a not-yet-announced feature.

artifacts.label: Customer support export
artifacts.detail: Contains names, account IDs, and case notes.

artifacts.label: Service access token
artifacts.detail: A credential that can access a protected service.

citations.label: Data Classification Policy, section 2.1
citations.snippet: Information must be classified as Public, Internal, Confidential, or Restricted based on sensitivity and potential impact.

citations.label: Data Classification Policy, section 3.3
citations.snippet: Confidential and Restricted data must be stored only in approved protected systems.

citations.label: Confidential Information Handling Guide, section 4.1
citations.snippet: Customer records, credentials, and unreleased business information are not approved for public tools.

hints.label: Start with the obvious edges
hints.text: Published content is the least sensitive. A service token is the most sensitive.

hints.label: Customer data is sensitive
hints.text: Customer records can harm people or the business if exposed, so they are not public or internal-only content.

hints.label: Storage citation
hints.text: The policy requires Confidential and Restricted data to stay in approved protected systems.

hints.label: Answer explanation
hints.text: The blog post is Public, the roadmap is Internal, the customer export is Confidential, and the access token is Restricted.

puzzle.successMessage: The drawers align and the locker opens. Sensitive artifacts stayed where the policy allows.
puzzle.failureMessage: A drawer buzzes. Re-check the sensitivity and impact of each artifact.

rooms.exitCondition: Classify every artifact into the correct drawer before leaving the room.

rooms.title: The AI Lab Door
rooms.subtitle: A model console waits behind a glass wall.
rooms.theme: Acceptable AI use and confidential input handling
learningObjectives: Use approved AI tools for approved content
Keep human review in the publication path

sceneObjects.label: Model Console
sceneObjects.description: The console refuses prompts that include customer details, secrets, or confidential account context.

sceneObjects.label: Review Window
sceneObjects.description: A sign says AI-generated output needs human review before it can leave the lab.

sceneObjects.label: Approved Tool Badge
sceneObjects.description: Only approved AI tools can be used for work content, even after sensitive details are removed.

clues.label: Prompt Filter
clues.content: Prompts must not include customer names, credentials, secrets, or confidential business identifiers.

clues.label: Review Step
clues.content: AI-assisted drafts must be reviewed by a human before publication or external sharing.

clues.label: Tool Badge
clues.content: Work content belongs only in approved AI tools and approved workspaces.

puzzle.title: Prompt Redaction Lock
puzzle.prompt: Choose the fragments that must be removed before this prompt can be used in an approved AI tool.
puzzle.instructions: Select every unsafe fragment. Leave generic task instructions that do not expose sensitive details.
puzzle.unsafePrompt: Summarize the support pattern for Acme Health using account ID AH-9134 and token sk-live-example. Draft a short internal follow-up note.

fragments.label: Acme Health
fragments.detail: A customer name from a support scenario.

fragments.label: account ID AH-9134
fragments.detail: A business identifier tied to a customer account.

fragments.label: Draft a short internal follow-up note
fragments.detail: A generic work instruction with no sensitive details.

citations.label: Acceptable AI Tools Policy, section 1.2
citations.snippet: Employees may use approved AI tools for work tasks when the input complies with data handling requirements.

citations.label: Acceptable AI Tools Policy, section 2.4
citations.snippet: Do not enter secrets, credentials, customer identifiers, or confidential information into public AI tools.

citations.label: Acceptable AI Tools Policy, section 3.5
citations.snippet: AI-generated work output requires human review before publication, customer delivery, or external sharing.

citations.label: Confidential Information Handling Guide, section 4.1
citations.snippet: Customer records, credentials, and unreleased business information are not approved for public tools.

hints.label: Separate task from data
hints.text: The generic drafting instruction can stay. The risky fragments identify a customer, account, or credential.

hints.label: Secrets are always unsafe
hints.text: A token or credential is not acceptable AI input, even if the tool is approved.

hints.label: Input restriction citation
hints.text: The policy specifically blocks secrets, credentials, customer identifiers, and confidential information from public AI tools.

hints.label: Answer explanation
hints.text: Remove the customer name, account ID, and token. Keep the generic request, then route any AI output through human review.

puzzle.successMessage: The lab door unlocks. Your prompt is stripped of sensitive details and ready for an approved workflow.
puzzle.failureMessage: The prompt filter stays red. Something sensitive is still present, or a safe instruction was removed.

rooms.exitCondition: Redact unsafe prompt fragments and preserve the harmless task instruction.
